2604.15877.title: Experience Compression Spectrum: Unifying Memory, Skills, and Rules in LLM Agents
2604.15877.paper_summary: As LLM agents scale to long-horizon, multi-session deployments, efficiently managing accumulated experience becomes a critical bottleneck. Agent memory systems and agent skill discovery both address this challenge -- extracting reusable knowledge from interaction traces -- yet a citation analysis of 1,136 references across 22 primary papers reveals a cross-community citation rate below 1%. We propose the \emph{Experience Compression Spectrum}, a unifying framework that positions memory, skills, and rules as points along a single axis of increasing compression (5--20$\times$ for episodic memory, 50--500$\times$ for procedural skills, 1,000$\times$+ for declarative rules), directly reducing context consumption, retrieval latency, and compute overhead. Mapping 20+ systems onto this spectrum reveals that every system operates at a fixed, predetermined compression level -- none supports adaptive cross-level compression, a gap we term the \emph{missing diagonal}. We further show that specialization alone is insufficient -- both communities independently solve shared sub-problems without exchanging solutions -- that evaluation methods are tightly coupled to compression levels, that transferability increases with compression at the cost of specificity, and that knowledge lifecycle management remains largely neglected. We articulate open problems and design principles for scalable, full-spectrum agent learning systems.
2604.15877.paper_summary_zh: 隨著 LLM 代理在長期、多會話的部署中擴展，有效管理累積的經驗成為一個關鍵瓶頸。代理記憶系統和代理技能發現都針對這一挑戰——從互動痕跡中提取可重用的知識——然而對 22 篇主要論文中 1,136 篇參考文獻的引用分析顯示，跨社群的引用率低於 1%。我們提出了 \emph{經驗壓縮光譜}，這是一個統一框架，將記憶、技能和規則定位於一個隨著壓縮程度增加的單一軸線上（對於情節記憶為 5--20$\times$，對於程序技能為 50--500$\times$，對於陳述性規則為 1,000$\times$ 以上），直接減少上下文消耗、檢索延遲和計算開銷。將 20 多個系統映射到這個光譜上顯示，每個系統都在固定的、預定的壓縮水平上運作——沒有一個支持自適應的跨級壓縮，我們稱這一空白為 \emph{缺失的對角線}。我們進一步表明，單靠專業化是不夠的——兩個社群獨立解決共享的子問題而不交換解決方案——評估方法與壓縮水平緊密相關，轉移性隨著壓縮而增加，但特異性則降低，並且知識生命周期管理仍然在很大程度上被忽視。我們闡明了可擴展的全光譜代理學習系統的開放問題和設計原則。
2604.15877.author: Xing Zhang et.al.
2604.15877.authors: Xing Zhang, Guanghui Wang, Yanwei Cui, Wei Qiu, Ziyuan Li, Bing Zhu, Peiyang He
